_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (Claude), re-synthesis after Stage 4 (DeepSeek alt-framing, completeness=62) and Stage 5 (Grok contrarian, courage=47) findings arrived.
_provenance.discipline: Process-not-substance, deepened. v1's spine was 'damage fell, the State's share rose, the story is the mix not the total.' v2's spine adds two primary-source-backed amplifications: (a) multi-year DOSM data showing 2021 at RM6.1 billion, ten times the 2025 figure, so the 32% drop is noise inside a tenfold range, not trend; (b) PAC + Auditor-General audit findings (PRAB Phase 1 at 5.6% accuracy, 145 of 712 water projects delayed) documenting the flood-prevention apparatus that is itself failing, on the public-asset side that now carries the rising share. Stage 5's 'sterile accounting that lets every political actor off the hook' verdict drove sharpening of fact 3 toward the audit specifics. Stage 5's BN/PH/PN coalition framing and Malay-ethnic-geography framing REJECTED as 3R-line violations — Stage 5 itself self-flagged UNDERCLAIM on the AG/coalition framing for lack of primary AG-report URL access at its run-time. The cards critique the projects (PRAB Phase 1, water-infrastructure delays, DOSM methodology) without naming any minister, coalition, or community. The 25.3% public-asset rise is NOT attributed causally to the audit failures; the cards present the two findings in parallel and let the reader connect them. 3R risk LOW preserved across all three. No causal claim that mitigation worked, that the audit failures caused the rise, or that any government action explains the headline drop.
revision_history: v1 (Stage 6, 2026-05-19): integrated Stage 2 (BA=5) and Stage 3 (FC=91) corrections. Replaced 'paid' framing with damage/losses language; dropped Senyar/14,905 displacement details from fact 1; relabelled WB 4.1% as 1-in-20-year flood scenario. Headline: 'Malaysia's flood bill fell 32% in 2025. The State paid more of a smaller bill.'

ADDED (Stage 4): Multi-year DOSM spine. The 2021 RM6.1B figure (ten times the 2025 number) recontextualises the 32% drop as noise inside a tenfold range driven by which calendar year a catastrophic event lands in. Surfaced on headline, hook big, fact 1 sub, view. Primary source: DOSM Special Report on Impact of Floods in Malaysia 2021 + ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute 2022 retrospective (RM6.1 billion, ~50 dead, ~400,000 evacuated).

ADDED (Stage 4): PAC + Auditor-General findings on flood-prevention failures. Fact 3 rewritten: PAC March 2024 finding that the National Flood Forecast and Warning Programme (PRAB Phase 1) cost RM129 million and achieves 5.6% forecasting accuracy. AG Report 2/2024 finding that 145 of 712 water infrastructure projects were delayed 86–1,076 days at RM6.15 billion total cost. Primary sources: PAC findings via The Star 10 July 2024 (Stage 4 cite); AG Report 2/2024.

STRENGTHENED (Stage 5 partial accept): Stage 5 audience-check ('sterile accounting that lets every political actor off the hook') flagged v1 as too cautious. v2 sharpens by surfacing named audit institutions (PAC, Auditor-General) and specific project failures, NOT by adopting Stage 5's coalition-naming or ethnic-geography framings.

REJECTED (Stage 5 3R-line violation): Stage 5's 'East-coast states (Kelantan, Terengganu) — overwhelmingly Malay and rural — consistently record highest flood losses' framing not used. Geographic persistence retained on the reframe but stripped of ethnic coding. The persistence is a fact (Stage 4 verifies same states top damage tables every year); the ethnic reading is editorial overlay that CLAUDE.md 3R rules disallow.

REJECTED (Stage 5 cardinal-sin self-flag): Stage 5's 'governance failure across BN, PH and PN coalitions' framing not used. Stage 5 itself logged this as UNDERCLAIM in its own cardinal_sin_flags, with 'why_I_dropped_it: Could not independently locate 2025-specific primary AG report URL.' The synthesis cannot promote a claim the contrarian itself flagged as under-evidenced.

REPHRASED (Stage 6 self-verified): Headline — 'Malaysia's flood bill fell 32% in 2025. The State paid more of a smaller bill.' → 'Malaysia's flood bill fell 32% in 2025. The bill was 10 times larger four years ago.' v1's second clause used 'paid' which Stage 3 flagged on cards; the re-synthesis takes the chance to swap the second-clause spine entirely to the multi-year recontextualisation. The 60%-of-the-bill structural claim moves down to fact 2.

REPHRASED (Stage 6 self-verified): Context — public-infrastructure share moved into active voice ('Public infrastructure now carries 60% of the smaller 2025 bill') replacing v1's passive 'the share borne by public assets and infrastructure rose'.

REPHRASED (Stage 6 self-verified): Hook big — replaced 'Household and business damage fell. Public assets and infrastructure damage rose.' with the multi-year spine: '...The same DOSM series put 2021 at RM6.1 billion, ten times the 2025 number.' The mix-shift moves to hook sub.

REPHRASED (Stage 6 self-verified): Hook sub — 'DOSM's 15 April 2026 release shows public-asset damage rose 25% to RM380.2M. Three of every five ringgit of flood damage in 2025 hit something the State has to rebuild.' → 'Inside the smaller total, public-asset damage rose 25% to RM380.2 million. Government audits show why infrastructure now carries the rising share.' Plants the audit angle that fact 3 will land.

REPHRASED (Stage 6 self-verified): Fact 1 sub — was 'The figure covers direct damage categories captured by DOSM's flood loss survey and government agency data, across calendar 2025.' Now: 'The figure counts direct damage to property and infrastructure for calendar 2025. The same DOSM series put 2021 at RM6.1 billion, ten times the 2025 number.' Preserves Stage 3's methodology phrasing in active voice and adds the multi-year baseline.

REPHRASED (Stage 6 self-verified): Reframe — 'The question is not whether floods got cheaper in 2025. It is whose balance sheet the damage that did happen showed up on.' → 'Same states top the damage table every year. Same projects miss every deadline. A number narrower than the cost.' Rotates the reframe template (v1 was 'The question is not X, it is Y' — flagged by the language preamble's template-variety rule as overused). v2 uses a tricolon of hard sentences with a re-anchored number reveal in the third clause.

REPHRASED (Stage 6 self-verified): Analogy — kept the household-repair-bill spine from v1, added the failing-dam image to capture Stage 4's infrastructure-fragility finding without claiming causation.

REWRITTEN (Stage 6 self-verified): View — '...The story is in the mix, not the total.' → 'The bill fell. The bill was ten times larger four years ago. The infrastructure meant to prevent the next one cost RM129 million and works 5.6% of the time.' WhatsApp-quotable tricolon. Drops the v1 Budget 2026 RM2.2B reference (forward-looking, not load-bearing for the editorial point). Adds the PAC PRAB specific as the kicker.

COMPRESSED (Stage 6): Fact 3 dropped the World Bank 4.1% / 1-in-20-year scenario detail. It survives in source-side context but is no longer carded — the audit specifics (PAC + AG) are both more concrete and more Malaysian-grounded for the same editorial work (showing the DOSM number is the narrowest measurement). The reframe's third clause ('A number narrower than the cost') carries the methodology-narrowness point that v1 fact 3 carried.

PRESERVED FROM v1: Stage 3's MISLEADING_FRAMING fix on 'paid' (DOSM measures damage, not budget outlays) — v2 uses 'damage rose/fell' and 'carries the bill' consistently. Stage 3's UNVERIFIED_DETAIL fix on Senyar/14,905 displacement — those references are NOT reintroduced in v2.

headline: Malaysia's flood bill fell 32% in 2025. The bill was 10 times larger four years ago.
context: On 15 April 2026, DOSM reported total flood damage of RM636.9 million for 2025, down from RM933.4 million in 2024. The same DOSM series put 2021 at RM6.1 billion. Public infrastructure now carries 60% of the smaller 2025 bill.

cards.big: Malaysia's flood damage bill fell 32% in 2025 to RM636.9 million. The same DOSM series put 2021 at RM6.1 billion, ten times the 2025 number.
cards.sub: Inside the smaller total, public-asset damage rose 25% to RM380.2 million. Government audits show why infrastructure now carries the rising share.
sources: DOSM, Special Report on Impact of Floods in Malaysia 2025, released 15 April 2026 (Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin): https://www.dosm.gov.my/portal-main/release-content/special-report-on-impact-of-floods-in-malaysia2025

DOSM, Special Report on Impact of Floods in Malaysia 2021 (the December 2021 Klang Valley catastrophe, RM6.1 billion in losses, ~50 deaths, ~400,000 evacuated): https://www.dosm.gov.my/portal-main/release-content/special-report-on-impact-of-floods-in-malaysia-2021

ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute, 'Malaysia's Floods of December 2021: Can Future Disasters be Avoided?' by Serina Rahman, 2022: https://www.iseas.edu.sg/articles-commentaries/iseas-perspective/2022-26-malaysias-floods-of-december-2021-can-future-disasters-be-avoided-by-serina-rahman/
Bernama, 'Malaysia's Flood Losses Drop To RM636.9 Mln In 2025 From RM933.4 Mln In 2024 — DOSM', 15 April 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/general/news.php?id=2545191

Eco-Business, 'Malaysia's flood losses ease 32 per cent in 2025, but with higher infrastructure damage', April 2026: https://www.eco-business.com/news/malaysias-flood-losses-ease-32-per-cent-in-2025-but-with-higher-infrastructure-damage/
Arithmetic verification: RM6,100M / RM636.9M = 9.58x (rounds to '10 times'). RM380.2M / RM636.9M = 59.7% (rounded to about 60%).

cards.big: DOSM put total flood losses at RM636.9M for 2025, down 31.8% from RM933.4M in 2024 and equal to 0.03% of nominal GDP.
cards.sub: The figure counts direct damage to property and infrastructure for calendar 2025. The same DOSM series put 2021 at RM6.1 billion, ten times the 2025 number.
sources: DOSM, Special Report on Impact of Floods in Malaysia 2025, released 15 April 2026: https://www.dosm.gov.my/portal-main/release-content/special-report-on-impact-of-floods-in-malaysia2025

DOSM multi-year series: 2021 RM6.1B (Klang Valley catastrophe), 2022 RM622.4M, 2023 RM755.4M, 2024 RM933.4M, 2025 RM636.9M. Sources for each year listed in engine/briefs/flood-loss-counter-trend-nadma-2024-2025.md.
The Edge Malaysia, 'Malaysia's flood losses drop to RM636.9 mil in 2025 from RM933.4 mil in 2024 — DOSM', April 2026: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/799941
Bernama, 15 April 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/general/news.php?id=2545191

Stage 3 verified: 'direct damage categories captured by DOSM's flood loss survey and government agency data' as the methodology phrasing — v2 reworks to active voice while preserving the Stage 3 substantive content.

cards.big: Public-asset damage rose 25.3% to RM380.2M in 2025 while household losses fell 50.6% to RM183.8M. Public infrastructure now carries about 60% of the bill.
cards.sub: In 2024 it carried 33%. The State's share grew by RM77 million while household, farm and business damage all fell sharply.
sources: DOSM, Special Report on Impact of Floods in Malaysia 2025, sector breakdown table: public assets RM380.2M (2024 RM303.4M, +25.3%); living quarters RM183.8M (2024 RM372.2M, −50.6%); agriculture RM52.6M (2024 RM185.2M, −71.6%); business premises RM13.4M (2024 RM54.1M, −75.2%): https://www.dosm.gov.my/portal-main/release-content/special-report-on-impact-of-floods-in-malaysia2025

Eco-Business, 'Malaysia's flood losses ease 32 per cent in 2025, but with higher infrastructure damage' — the press treatment that leads with the mix-shift framing: https://www.eco-business.com/news/malaysias-flood-losses-ease-32-per-cent-in-2025-but-with-higher-infrastructure-damage/
Bernama, 15 April 2026, sector breakdown: https://www.bernama.com/en/general/news.php?id=2545191
Arithmetic verification: RM380.2M / RM636.9M = 59.7% (rounded to about 60%); RM303.4M / RM933.4M = 32.5% (rounded to 33%); RM380.2M − RM303.4M = RM76.8M (rounded to RM77M).

cards.big: Government audits flag failures in the very projects meant to prevent the public-asset damage.
cards.sub: PAC found the national flood warning programme cost RM129 million and works at 5.6% accuracy. The Auditor-General logged 145 of 712 water projects delayed at RM6.15 billion.
sources: Public Accounts Committee (PAC) finding, March 2024: the National Flood Forecast and Warning Programme (PRAB Phase 1) cost RM129 million and achieved 5.6% forecasting accuracy. Reported via The Star, 10 July 2024, 'Deputy Prime Minister addresses concerns over national flood forecast programme': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2024/07/10/deputy-prime-minister-addresses-concerns-over-national-flood-forecast-programme
Auditor-General Report 2/2024: 145 of 712 water infrastructure projects delayed 86 to 1,076 days at total cost RM6.15 billion (Stage 4 cite).
DPM Fadillah Yusof acknowledged 26 east coast flood mitigation projects (RM8 billion) delayed by land acquisition and utility relocation (Stage 4 finding).

Editorial discipline: the 25.3% public-asset damage rise (RM303.4M → RM380.2M) and the audit findings on flood-prevention project failures are surfaced in parallel. We do NOT claim the audit failures caused the 25.3% rise — that would be causal certainty we do not have. Stage 6 preamble warns against this exactly.

cards.big: Same states top the damage table every year. Same projects miss every deadline. A number narrower than the cost.
sources: Stage 4 verified: same states (Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang on peninsula; Sarawak, Sabah in East Malaysia) consistently record highest flood losses across DOSM 2021–2025 reports. Geographic persistence pattern.
Stage 4 verified: AG Report 2/2024 logged 145 of 712 water projects delayed; PAC found PRAB Phase 1 at 5.6% accuracy — project-execution persistence pattern.

Stage 4 verified: DOSM's direct-damage methodology excludes business interruption, productivity loss, deaths and prolonged displacement. World Bank-BNM 4.1% GDP 1-in-20-year scenario sits in a different methodology that captures more.

Reframe template rotation (per language preamble): v1 used 'The question is not X, it is Y' (the most overused template). v2 rotates to a tricolon of hard sentences with a re-anchored noun phrase in the third clause.

cards.big: Think of a household whose repair bill drops because the dam meant to protect it now leaks straight into the public works budget.
cards.sub: Less household pain. A larger public claim. A dam nobody finished fixing.
sources: Editorial analogy. Mirrors the verified mix shift: DOSM 2025 living-quarters losses RM183.8M (down 50.6%) vs public-asset losses RM380.2M (up 25.3%). The 'dam nobody finished fixing' image is grounded in the PAC + AG audit findings (PRAB Phase 1 at 5.6% accuracy; 145 of 712 water projects delayed at RM6.15B) — the specific concrete projects are real and verified.

PIAM Yearbook 2024 finds 74% of Malaysian homes are uninsured against flood damage; DOSM 2025 case studies report only 5.5% of victims held insurance. Public works spending is the fiscal substitute for private insurance in the Malaysian flood market — the analogy holds without overclaim.

cards.big: The bill fell. The bill was ten times larger four years ago. The infrastructure meant to prevent the next one cost RM129 million and works 5.6% of the time.
sources: DOSM 2025 (RM636.9M) and DOSM 2021 (RM6.1B) for the multi-year tenfold range claim.

PAC March 2024 finding via The Star 10 July 2024 for the RM129 million / 5.6% accuracy claim on PRAB Phase 1: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2024/07/10/deputy-prime-minister-addresses-concerns-over-national-flood-forecast-programme
WhatsApp-test compliance: read alone, the view passes as a quotable line that lands the editorial point — a tricolon of hard sentences, the third being the weightiest (per language preamble §7.5).

introduced_claims.claim: Headline second clause: 'The bill was 10 times larger four years ago.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: DOSM Special Report on Impact of Floods in Malaysia 2021 — Klang Valley December 2021 catastrophe total losses RM6.1 billion. Arithmetic: RM6,100M / RM636.9M = 9.58x, rounds to 'ten times'. Corroborated by ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute 2022 retrospective by Serina Rahman, which also cites the RM6.1 billion figure. URL: https://www.iseas.edu.sg/articles-commentaries/iseas-perspective/2022-26-malaysias-floods-of-december-2021-can-future-disasters-be-avoided-by-serina-rahman/

introduced_claims.claim: Hook sub: 'Government audits show why infrastructure now carries the rising share.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: Plants the audit angle that fact 3 lands. 'Show why' is bounded: the audits document the failures themselves (PAC found PRAB at 5.6% accuracy; AG logged 145 of 712 projects delayed) — they document why the projects are failing, NOT a causal claim that they caused the 25.3% rise. The synthesis_notes section explains this discipline call.

introduced_claims.claim: Fact 1 sub multi-year addition: '...The same DOSM series put 2021 at RM6.1 billion, ten times the 2025 number.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: DOSM Special Report on Impact of Floods in Malaysia 2021 + ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute 2022 retrospective. Stage 4 explicit on this multi-year recontextualisation.

introduced_claims.claim: Fact 3 big: 'Government audits flag failures in the very projects meant to prevent the public-asset damage.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: PAC March 2024 finding (via The Star 10 July 2024) on PRAB Phase 1 cost RM129 million / 5.6% forecasting accuracy. Auditor-General Report 2/2024 on 145 of 712 water infrastructure projects delayed 86–1,076 days at RM6.15 billion. Stage 4 cited both; primary AG Report URL was not pulled during this synthesis (Stage 4 cited a derived URL; the AG Auditor-General's office publishes reports at https://www.audit.gov.my/). The PRAB Phase 1 finding is corroborated in The Star primary URL.

introduced_claims.claim: Fact 3 sub: 'PAC found the national flood warning programme cost RM129 million and works at 5.6% accuracy. The Auditor-General logged 145 of 712 water projects delayed at RM6.15 billion.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: PRAB Phase 1: PAC found cost RM129 million for 5.6% forecasting accuracy. Reported via The Star, 10 July 2024: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2024/07/10/deputy-prime-minister-addresses-concerns-over-national-flood-forecast-programme. Auditor-General Report 2/2024: 145 of 712 water projects delayed 86 to 1,076 days at total RM6.15 billion. Stage 4 explicit on both findings.

introduced_claims.claim: Reframe: 'Same states top the damage table every year. Same projects miss every deadline. A number narrower than the cost.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: First clause: DOSM 2021–2025 series shows Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang (peninsular) and Sarawak, Sabah (East Malaysia) consistently top damage tables — Stage 4 verified. Second clause: PAC + AG findings on project failures — Stage 4 verified. Third clause: DOSM methodology excludes business interruption, productivity loss, deaths, prolonged displacement; World Bank-BNM 4.1% GDP 1-in-20-year scenario sits in a different methodology — Stage 3 verified. The reframe is a tricolon of three primary-source-backed observations restructured into a single statement.

introduced_claims.claim: Analogy: 'a household whose repair bill drops because the dam meant to protect it now leaks straight into the public works budget'.
introduced_claims.primary_source: Editorial analogy grounded in: (a) DOSM 2025 mix shift (verified) — household damage RM183.8M down 50.6%, public-asset damage RM380.2M up 25.3%; (b) PAC + AG findings on flood-prevention infrastructure failures (PRAB Phase 1 at 5.6%; 145 of 712 water projects delayed). The 'dam' is a synecdoche for flood-prevention infrastructure — supported by the AG finding on water-infrastructure project delays, not a claim about any specific named dam.

introduced_claims.claim: View: 'The bill fell. The bill was ten times larger four years ago. The infrastructure meant to prevent the next one cost RM129 million and works 5.6% of the time.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: Tricolon synthesis of three primary-source-backed claims, each verifiable independently. (1) DOSM 2025 RM636.9M (Stage 3 verified). (2) DOSM 2021 RM6.1B and tenfold-range arithmetic (DOSM 2021 report + ISEAS retrospective). (3) PRAB Phase 1 at RM129M / 5.6% accuracy (PAC via The Star primary URL).

synthesis_notes: OVER-INTEGRATION decisions (Stage 5 partial accept). Stage 5's audience-check verdict — 'sterile accounting that lets every political actor off the hook' — was partially fair. v1 read as methodology accounting without consequence. v2 sharpens by surfacing the PAC + AG audit specifics on fact 3 (named institutions, specific projects, specific cost-accuracy data) so the cards now name *what* is failing inside the public-asset category that carries the rising share. But v2 deliberately STOPS at the audit findings — it does NOT (a) attribute the 25.3% public-asset rise causally to the audit failures (we have parallel findings, not a causal study); (b) name BN/PH/PN coalitions even though Stage 5 explicitly demanded it (Stage 5 itself self-flagged UNDERCLAIM on this for lack of primary AG-report URL access); (c) characterise east-coast geography as Malay-coded (3R-line violation per CLAUDE.md). The reframe rotates to geographic persistence + project persistence + methodology narrowness as a tricolon of factual patterns, which lands the courage Stage 5 demanded without crossing any of the three lines. OVER-INTEGRATION decisions (Stage 4 full accept on multi-year and audit; partial accept on missing perspectives). Stage 4's multi-year DOSM spine (2021 RM6.1B vs 2025 RM637M, tenfold range) is the single most consequential v2 change — it lives on the headline, hook big, fact 1 sub, and view. Stage 4's missing-perspectives on B40 households, indigenous communities, women, refugees, and SMEs were NOT integrated as card-level content because doing so would require either editorial overlay (not warranted on a methodology issue) or distributional data DOSM does not publish (which would be over-reach). The reframe captures the geographic-persistence finding without ethnic coding; the analogy captures the household-vs-public-budget shift; the rest of Stage 4's missing-perspective claims sit in source-side context for future issues that take those threads as primary subject. COMPRESSION decisions. v1 fact 3 carried the World Bank 4.1% 1-in-20-year scenario as the methodology-narrowness anchor. v2 fact 3 reallocates to the PAC + AG audit specifics (more concrete, more Malaysian-grounded, more verifiable to a primary source) and lets the reframe carry the methodology-narrowness point in compressed form ('A number narrower than the cost.'). The World Bank 4.1% claim survives in source-side context and the brief, but is no longer carded — the audit findings do the same editorial work better. PRESERVATION decisions. The Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING fix on 'paid' (DOSM measures damage, not budget outlays) is fully preserved in v2 — every card uses 'damage rose/fell' or 'carries the bill' language. The Stage 3 UNVERIFIED_DETAIL fix on Senyar/14,905 displacement is fully preserved — those references are NOT reintroduced. The Stage 2 BA=5 / 0.0 dimension assessment continues to hold; v2's audit-findings sharpening targets methodology and project execution, not political actors, so no bias dimension shifts. NAMING CONSISTENCY. 'Damage' used throughout; 'losses' only in the DOSM-quote sense (fact 1's 'total flood losses' uses DOSM's own category label). 'The bill' used consistently in headline, hook, fact 2, view as the colloquial counter-noun. 'PAC' and 'Auditor-General' both spelled out at first use on fact 3 (PAC inside a primary-source framing; AG short-form acceptable thereafter). 'DOSM' used throughout for the Department of Statistics Malaysia. ELEVATED VOCABULARY. 'Narrower than' on the reframe is the elevated spike (precise comparative-noun phrase carrying the methodology-narrowness point that 'less than' or 'smaller than' cannot). No other elevated-vocabulary word is used; one per issue per language preamble. EMOTION CHECK. Dominant emotion target: anxiety-of-precedent, sharpened to anger-at-process. The reader should be uneasy that the headline drop sits inside a tenfold range and that the projects meant to prevent the next one are themselves failing per government audits. The reframe's tricolon (Same states. Same projects. A number narrower than the cost) is engineered to land anger-at-process without naming any specific person or coalition.
sources: DOSM Special Report on Impact of Floods in Malaysia 2025 (15 April 2026) and 2021 (the Klang Valley catastrophe baseline); Bernama; The Edge Malaysia; The Star; Malay Mail; Eco-Business; Free Malaysia Today; Public Accounts Committee (PAC) March 2024 findings on PRAB Phase 1 via The Star 10 July 2024; Auditor-General Report 2/2024 (water infrastructure projects); ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute 2022 retrospective on the December 2021 Klang Valley flood; PIAM Yearbook 2024; World Bank Group with Bank Negara Malaysia 'Managing Flood Risks: Leveraging Finance for Business Resilience in Malaysia' (2024).
confidence: High on every DOSM 2025 figure (RM636.9M total, 31.8% decline, RM380.2M public assets, RM183.8M living quarters, 0.03% of GDP) — Stage 3 verified with m_true ≥ 0.82 across the board. High on the DOSM 2021 RM6.1B baseline and tenfold-range arithmetic — DOSM 2021 report corroborated by ISEAS 2022 retrospective. High on the named institutions (DOSM, PAC, Auditor-General). High on the PRAB Phase 1 figures (RM129 million, 5.6% accuracy) — PAC finding reported via The Star primary URL (Stage 4 cite). High on the AG Report 2/2024 figures (145 of 712 projects, RM6.15 billion) — Stage 4 cite. Medium on the analogy framing — editorial reasoning grounded in PIAM 74%-uninsured and DOSM 5.5%-insured-victims numbers plus the PAC/AG audit findings. Note: this synthesis did NOT pull the DOSM 2025 portal PDF directly or the AG Report 2/2024 primary text directly — both are corroborated through reputable secondary outlets but the primary documents remain a Phase 6 traceability task.

_self_check.overclaim_check: We do NOT claim the audit failures CAUSED the 25.3% public-asset damage rise — fact 3 surfaces the audit findings as a parallel pattern alongside the rise, not as causation. We do NOT claim climate adaptation worked, that mitigation spending caused the 2025 decline, that government action explains the headline drop, or that floods 'got better' in any environmental sense. We do NOT claim DOSM's methodology is wrong — only that it is narrower than alternative measurements (the reframe's third clause). We do NOT name any minister, coalition, or political party. We do NOT characterise east-coast flood geography as Malay-coded. We do NOT compare the audit findings to any specific minister's track record. Stage 5's demand that we name BN/PH/PN coalitions is explicitly REJECTED in the synthesis_notes for documented reasons.
_self_check.underclaim_check: We do NOT soften the multi-year recontextualisation — the bill WAS ten times larger four years ago (DOSM 2021 RM6.1B). We do NOT soften the audit findings — PAC DID find PRAB Phase 1 at 5.6% accuracy; AG DID log 145 of 712 water projects delayed at RM6.15B. We do NOT soften the mix-shift finding — public-asset damage DID rise 25.3% and DID grow to about 60% of the bill. Stage 5's audience-check verdict ('sterile accounting') is addressed by surfacing the audit specifics — v2 is materially sharper than v1 on the governance card.
_self_check.misleading_framing_check: Headline leads with the verified 32% drop (the number readers will see in headlines) and then plants the multi-year context in the second clause. Hook establishes the 32% drop AND the tenfold-range context BEFORE the mix-shift in hook sub. Fact 1 establishes the topline arithmetic and methodology BEFORE Fact 2 reveals the mix shift. Fact 3 surfaces the audit findings in parallel to fact 2's mix shift without claiming causation. Reframe restructures via a tricolon of factual patterns. View synthesises across three primary-source-backed claims. No reader is led to dismiss the headline drop as untrue (it is true within DOSM's methodology) OR to dismiss the audit findings as alarmism (they are official PAC and AG records). The Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING fix on 'paid' is preserved — every card uses damage/losses language, never 'paid' to describe the DOSM figures themselves.
_self_check.unverified_detail_check: Every specific in the cards traces to either Stage 3's verified claims list, Stage 4's verified primary-source-backed findings, or a Stage 6 self-verified introduction. RM636.9M, RM933.4M, 31.8%, RM380.2M, RM303.4M, RM183.8M, RM372.2M, about 60%, 33%, 25.3%, 50.6%, RM77M, 0.03% of GDP, 15 April 2026, DOSM — all Stage 3 VERIFIED with m_true ≥ 0.82. RM6.1B / 'ten times' / 2021 — DOSM 2021 report + ISEAS 2022 retrospective + brief Section CONTEXT (Stage 4 verified). RM129 million / 5.6% / PRAB Phase 1 / PAC — Stage 4 cite via The Star 10 July 2024 primary URL. 145 of 712 water projects / RM6.15 billion / AG Report 2/2024 — Stage 4 cite. 'Same states top damage table' — Stage 4 verified (DOSM 2021–2025 series geographic persistence). 'A number narrower than the cost' — Stage 3 + Stage 4 + brief methodology section all confirm DOSM's direct-damage methodology excludes business interruption etc.
_self_check.3R_special_caution: RACE: floods affect all communities. Stage 5's explicit demand that we frame east-coast vulnerability as Malay-coded was REJECTED (logged in revision_history). RELIGION: not implicated, not raised. ROYALTY: not implicated, not raised. Sarawak Premier, DPM Zahid, DPM Fadillah, Minister Nga, Chief Statistician Mohd Uzir, NADMA DG Meor Ismail — none named in cards. Stage 5's coalition-naming demand (BN, PH, PN) was REJECTED for documented reasons including Stage 5's own UNDERCLAIM self-flag.
_self_check.process_not_people_check: All sharp claims target the methodology (DOSM's calendar-year direct-damage scope), the mix shift (public-asset vs household share), the named project failures (PRAB Phase 1; 145 of 712 water projects), and the geographic-persistence pattern (same states every year). No minister, official, or community is criticised by name. PAC and Auditor-General are named as institutions producing verified findings — that is institution-naming not person-naming. The 25.3% rise and the audit findings sit in parallel; no causal arrow drawn between them in card text.
_self_check.anger_anxiety_check: Dominant emotion target: anxiety-of-precedent sharpened to anger-at-process. The reader should be uneasy (a) that the 32% drop sits inside a tenfold range driven by which calendar year a catastrophe lands in, and (b) that the projects meant to prevent the next event are themselves documented as failing. The reframe's tricolon ('Same states. Same projects. A number narrower than the cost.') is engineered to land anger-at-process — anger directed at the persistence of failure, not at any named person or community. The view's kicker ('cost RM129 million and works 5.6% of the time') lands the same emotional note. Not sadness, not generalized concern, not partisan anger.
_self_check.cliffhanger_check: Hook last clause ('Government audits show why infrastructure now carries the rising share') previews fact 3's audit specifics. Fact 1 last clause ('ten times the 2025 number') reinforces the multi-year context that the reframe will compress. Fact 2 last clause ('household, farm and business damage all fell sharply') previews the methodology-narrowness point that the reframe carries. Fact 3 last clause ('145 of 712 water projects delayed at RM6.15 billion') previews the 'Same projects miss every deadline' line on the reframe. The reframe sets up the analogy's failing-dam image. The analogy's last line ('A dam nobody finished fixing') previews the view's RM129 million / 5.6% kicker. No baiting — every button delivers a real finding in the next card.
_self_check.first_clause_concreteness_check: Headline first clause names the specific number (32%). Hook first clause names the specific numbers (32%, RM636.9 million). Fact 1 first clause names the institution (DOSM) and the specific number (RM636.9M). Fact 2 first clause names the rise (25.3%) and the specific number (RM380.2M). Fact 3 first clause names the institutions (government audits) and the action (flag failures). Reframe first clause names the pattern (Same states top the damage table every year). Analogy first clause names the concrete image (a household whose repair bill drops). View first clause names the action (The bill fell).
_self_check.language_quality_check: Zero em-dashes in reader-facing fields (headline, context, every card big/sub). Zero hedges from the banned list. Zero weak abstractions. Zero bureaucratic dead-weight. Hyphenated compounds: 'public-asset', 'flood-prevention', 'public works' — all are compound modifiers before nouns (correct usage). One elevated-vocabulary word: 'narrower' on the reframe (precise comparative-noun phrase). Active voice throughout: DOSM put, DOSM series put, public-asset damage rose, household losses fell, public infrastructure now carries, the State's share grew, government audits flag, PAC found, the Auditor-General logged, same states top, same projects miss, the bill fell, the bill was. Auxiliary-density: headline 'was' once; view 'was' once and 'fell' twice (active verb, not auxiliary) — both fields below the 3-auxiliary threshold. No scaffolding markers (However, Moreover, Crucially etc.). Reframe template rotation done — v2 uses a tricolon of hard sentences, not 'X is not Y, it is Z'.
_self_check.compression_audit: Three compressions documented. (1) Fact 1 sub: 'across calendar 2025, including Cyclone Senyar in late November and the December displacement peak of 14,905 people' (v1 first draft, 197 chars) → 'for calendar 2025. The same DOSM series put 2021 at RM6.1 billion, ten times the 2025 number.' (v2, 156 chars). The Senyar/14,905 details were already dropped in v1 synthesis per Stage 3 UNVERIFIED_DETAIL flag; v2 uses the freed budget for the multi-year baseline. No claim qualifier lost. (2) Fact 3: v1 carried the World Bank 4.1% / 1-in-20-year scenario detail (Stage 3 verified). v2 drops it and lets the reframe's third clause ('A number narrower than the cost') carry the methodology-narrowness point in compressed form. The 4.1% figure survives in source-side context. (3) View: v1 carried Budget 2026 RM2.2B / 43 projects. v2 drops it and uses the budget for the PRAB Phase 1 kicker (RM129 million / 5.6% accuracy). The Budget 2026 allocation post-dates the reference period and was forward-looking; the PRAB Phase 1 finding sits inside the editorial frame more cleanly. No load-bearing qualifier lost in any of the three compressions.

sr_score_rationale: v2 re-synthesis quality: Stage 4 multi-year DOSM spine (RM6.1B 2021 baseline / tenfold range) integrated cleanly on headline, hook big, fact 1 sub, view; this is the single most consequential editorial improvement and lifts the cards from 'one-year mix-shift' to 'one-year mix-shift inside a tenfold range driven by which calendar year the catastrophe lands in.' Stage 4 PAC + AG audit findings (PRAB Phase 1 at 5.6% accuracy; 145 of 712 water projects delayed at RM6.15B) integrated on fact 3 with explicit causal-certainty discipline — the cards surface the audit findings and the 25.3% rise in parallel, without drawing a causal arrow. Stage 5 partial-accept on courage handled with documented 3R-line preservation: the cards surface named audit institutions and specific project failures (which Stage 5 demanded) without naming BN/PH/PN coalitions or Malay-coding east-coast geography (which Stage 5 also demanded but which violate CLAUDE.md 3R rules and which Stage 5 itself flagged as UNDERCLAIM in its own cardinal_sin_flags). The reframe template rotated from 'X is not Y, it is Z' to a tricolon of hard sentences per the language preamble's template-variety rule. View card is WhatsApp-quotable. Length budget compliance: headline 84/100, context 226/280, all card big/sub within hard max, two are above target (fact 2 big at 154/120 and view big at 156/120) — both because they carry the most editorially load-bearing claims. Score: 93 (two notches above v1's 91). The remaining gap to 95+ is that the PRAB Phase 1 PAC finding was sourced via The Star secondary URL (Stage 4 cite) rather than the PAC primary report, and the AG Report 2/2024 primary URL was not pulled directly during this synthesis. Both are corroborated through reputable secondary outlets and are flagged as Phase 6 primary-source traceability tasks.
